details: The scheme "Aastha Yojana" was launched by the Department of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of Rajasthan. It aims to provide BPL-equivalent benefits to families with multiple specially-abled members. Under this scheme, eligible families receive free medical treatment, ration supplies, and other welfare benefits. To avail of the benefits, a family must possess an Aastha Card, and their annual income should not exceed ₹1.20 lakh.
benefits: Aastha cardholder families receive benefits similar to BPL families, including free medical treatment and ration supplies.
Aastha cardholders are entitled to benefits from all BPL-related schemes run by different state government departments.
eligibility: The applicant should be a native of Rajasthan.
The two or more members should be specially abled from the same family.
The applicant must have a disability of 40%.
The annual family income should not exceed ₹1,20,000/-.

offline: The interested applicant should visit office of the District Social Justice & Empowerment Office and request a hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the District Social Justice & Empowerment Office.

Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the District Social Justice & Empowerment Office to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission and a unique identification number (if applicable).

faqs.question: Who is eligible for the Aastha Scheme?
faqs.answer: Families with at least two specially-abled members (40% or more disability) and an annual income below ₹1,20,000 are eligible.

faqs.question: What benefits does the scheme provide?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides free medical services, ration supplies, and access to BPL-equivalent welfare schemes.

faqs.question: Is there any fee for applying for the Aastha Card?
faqs.answer: No, applying for the Aastha Card is completely free of cost.

faqs.question: Can an individual apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, this scheme is for families with at least two eligible members with disabilities.

faqs.question: Can Aastha Cardholders avail of other government schemes?
faqs.answer: Yes, they are eligible for all BPL-category welfare schemes run by various departments.

faqs.question: Whom should I contact for more information?
faqs.answer: The applicant can contact the District Social Justice & Empowerment Office for queries and application assistance.

faqs.question: Can I apply online for this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, the application process is offline.

faqs.question: Will the Aastha Card expire?
faqs.answer: The card remains valid as long as the eligibility criteria are met and for the latest update, contact the department.

faqs.question: Where can I apply for the Aastha Card?
faqs.answer: The applicant can apply at the District Social Justice & Empowerment Office in your district.

faqs.question: What documents are needed to apply?
faqs.answer: Need an Aadhaar Card, Residence Proof, Disability Certificate, etc.
